channel.title: Technology - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

  2. US hardware sales rose 69% in March 2026 following strong Switch 2 performance | US Monthly Charts  GamesIndustry.biz
  3. ‘Pokopia’ Flurry Helps Nintendo’s Switch 2 Top US Sales in March  Bloomberg.com
  4. U.S. video game spending jumped 12% YoY in March - Circana (EA:NASDAQ)  Seeking Alpha
  5. Marathon Was One Of March’s Best-Selling Games But That Probably Won’t Stop The Obsession Over Steam Charts  Kotaku

item.title: I went hands on with the new Framework Laptop Pro 13 — an upgradable laptop that finally feels top of the line - Tom's Guide
item.description:
1. I went hands on with the new Framework Laptop Pro 13 — an upgradable laptop that finally feels top of the line  Tom's Guide
  2. Framework Has a Better, More Take-Apartable Laptop  WIRED

  4. Framework announces Laptop 13 Pro, ‘the MacBook Pro for Linux users’  The Verge
  5. Framework is building an eGPU kit for its Laptop 16  Engadget

item.title: Fallout: New Vegas dev claims Bethesda made a PowerPoint of everything Obsidian did 'wrong' - Polygon.com
item.description:
1. Fallout: New Vegas dev claims Bethesda made a PowerPoint of everything Obsidian did 'wrong'  Polygon.com
  2. Bethesda doesn’t have ‘the engineering know-how’ to remaster Fallout New Vegas, former Obsidian CCO says  Video Games Chronicle
  3. Fallout: New Vegas' DLC Was Meant To To Set Up Sequel Game  80 Level
  4. Fallout: New Vegas dev says it could've been set in New Orleans because 'the vibe was so cool, the flavor was cool, [it would've been] so sweet'  Yahoo Tech
  5. "Those folks came to Bethesda and said, 'We're gonna have a fun lawsuit'": Fallout New Vegas devs "got in trouble" after forgetting about trademarks for some material, says Obsidian veteran  GamesRadar+
